_comment: Illustrative LLM output for Founder Weekly Review.

reflection.week_headline: Strong shipping week, but PM is the bottleneck again
reflection.key_pattern: For the second consecutive week, you were the last person to unblock the team — not the first. Alex and David were blocked by PM deliverables (design spec, API contract) and you caught it at end-of-week. This isn't a scheduling failure; it's a role clarity failure. The team needs PM input at the start of the sprint, not as a blocker mid-sprint.
reflection.leverage_point: Before next sprint planning ends, walk through every active work stream and ask: 'What do you need from me in the next 3 days?' Make this a standing check-in item, not a reactive catch.
reflection.metric_commentary: MRR +3.2% week-over-week on a compounding trajectory is healthy. Support tickets dropping 26% (31→23) while signups grew is the most interesting signal — it suggests the recent product changes are reducing friction, not creating it. Watch this ratio for 3 more weeks before drawing conclusions.
reflection.decision_quality_note: The pricing update deferral shows good prioritisation discipline — you identified it would have created too much rework mid-quarter. The James Kim hire is high-confidence and irreversible — make sure the onboarding plan is written before Aug 1, not scrambled on the day.
reflection.energy_note: Medium energy on a week with 3 wins and a board deck ahead of schedule signals you're running on reserves, not surplus. The 3 hours spent on Slack/email is worth addressing — batching async communication is not a productivity hack, it's a leadership behaviour that teaches the team what 'urgent' means.
reflection.next_week_intention: Be the unblocking force before the team hits a wall, not after.
reflection.question_to_sit_with: If I removed myself from every meeting that didn't require a decision from me this week, what would I get done?
